name: RHS Nereus (Y 4)
events: 15 Jun 1942 Nereus (Lt.Cdr. Rallis) sank a sailing vessel with gunfire off Scarpanto.
15 Jul 1942 Nereus (Lt.Cdr. Rallis) sank three sailing vessels with gunfire off Karpathos.

19 Jul 1942 While on patrol of Karpathos, Nereus (Lt.Cdr. Rallis) fires four torpedoes at what is thought to be a 6000 ton merchant. In reality it was the Italian hospital ship Sicilia (9646 GRT). All torpedoes missed the target.
5 Aug 1942 While on patrol of Rhodes, Greece Nereus attacked the German merchant Wachtfels (8467 GRT). All torpedoed fired missed their target.
6 Aug 1942 Nereus (Lt.Cdr. Rallis) sank a small Greek sailing vessel with gunfire off Rhodes.
24 Sep 1942 Nereus (Lt.Cdr. Rallis) torpedoed and sank the Italian Fiume (662 GRT) about 7 nautical miles south-west of Rhodes.

16 Feb 1944 In a signal timed 2130 A/16, the Capt. S.1 sent the following orders to the submarines mentioned; 1) RHS Nereus (Lt. A. Panagiotou) proceed to patrol in the vicinity Anaphi and Santorini between long. 25°00'E and 26°00'E. 2) HMS Unruly (Lt. J.P. Fyfe, RN) is not to proceed east 24°40'E. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
